# zig-keychain -- Complete API Reference

> Cross-platform keychain/secrets abstraction in Zig with C FFI -- macOS Keychain (SecItem) and Linux Secret Service (libsecret).

Published documentation: https://transscendsurvival.org/zig-keychain/. License: Zlib OR MIT.

## Installation

```
zig fetch --save git+https://github.com/Jesssullivan/zig-keychain.git
```

Then in build.zig:

```zig
const dep = b.dependency("zig_keychain", .{ .target = target, .optimize = optimize });
exe.root_module.addImport("zig-keychain", dep.module("zig-keychain"));
```

Or as a git submodule for C FFI consumers:

```
git submodule add https://github.com/Jesssullivan/zig-keychain.git vendor/keychain
cd vendor/keychain && zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast
```

Link (macOS): `-lzig-keychain -framework Security -framework CoreFoundation`

Link (Linux): `-lzig-keychain $(pkg-config --libs libsecret-1 glib-2.0)`

Include `#include "zig_keychain.h"`.

## Source Structure

- `src/root.zig` - Zig package API root
- `src/ffi.zig` - C FFI exports (4 functions)
- `src/keychain.zig` - Platform dispatch
- `src/keychain_macos.zig` - macOS Security.framework backend (SecItem)
- `src/keychain_linux.zig` - Linux libsecret backend (D-Bus Secret Service)
- `src/libsecret_bridge.c` - C bridge for libsecret varargs calls
- `src/libsecret_bridge.h` - Bridge header for Zig @cImport
- `include/zig_keychain.h` - C header (4 functions)
- `examples/store_lookup.c` - C usage example

## C API Reference (zig_keychain.h)

All buffers are caller-provided. Functions are thread-safe and stateless.

### Store

```c
int zig_keychain_store(
    const char *service, size_t service_len,
    const char *account, size_t account_len,
    const uint8_t *data, size_t data_len
);
```

Store a generic secret in the system keychain/secret store. Uses upsert semantics: an existing item with the same service+account pair is replaced. Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure.

- macOS: SecItemDelete + SecItemAdd (kSecClassGenericPassword)
- Linux: libsecret SecretValue binary store

### Lookup

```c
int zig_keychain_lookup(
    const char *service, size_t service_len,
    const char *account, size_t account_len,
    uint8_t *out, size_t out_capacity
);
```

Look up a generic secret from the system keychain/secret store by service name and account name. Copies the secret data into the caller-provided output buffer. Returns the number of bytes written on success, -1 if the item was not found, -2 on error (including buffer too small).

- macOS: SecItemCopyMatching (kSecClassGenericPassword, kSecReturnData)
- Linux: libsecret SecretValue binary lookup

### Delete

```c
int zig_keychain_delete(
    const char *service, size_t service_len,
    const char *account, size_t account_len
);
```

Delete a generic secret from the system keychain/secret store, matched by service name and account name. Returns 0 on success (including when the item does not exist), -1 on error.

- macOS: SecItemDelete
- Linux: secret_password_clear_sync

### Search

```c
int zig_keychain_search(
    const char *account, size_t account_len,
    char *out, size_t out_capacity
);
```

Search for keychain items whose account name matches the given value. Writes matching service names as null-separated strings into the caller-provided output buffer. Returns the number of matches found, or -1 on error.

- macOS: SecItemCopyMatching (kSecMatchLimitAll, kSecReturnAttributes)
- Linux: secret_service_search_sync

## Zig API Reference

### keychain.zig (platform dispatch)

- `Result` (union): `.success` ([]const u8 aliasing the caller-provided lookup buffer), `.not_found`, `.err` ([]const u8)
- `store(service: []const u8, account: []const u8, data: []const u8) !void`: Store a generic secret in the platform keychain.
- `lookup(service: []const u8, account: []const u8, out_buf: []u8) !Result`: Look up a generic secret into caller-provided memory.
- `delete(service: []const u8, account: []const u8) !void`: Delete a generic secret from the platform keychain.
- `search(account: []const u8, out_buf: [*]u8, out_capacity: usize) !usize`: Search for keychain items matching an account name. Returns match count.

### keychain_macos.zig (Security.framework)

- `store(service, account, data) !void`: SecItemDelete + SecItemAdd (kSecClassGenericPassword). Upsert semantics.
- `lookup(service, account, out_buf) !Result`: SecItemCopyMatching (kSecReturnData). Copies into caller-provided memory and returns `.success`, `.not_found`, or `.err`.
- `delete(service, account) !void`: SecItemDelete. Silent success on not-found.
- `search(account, out_buf, out_capacity) !usize`: SecItemCopyMatching (kSecMatchLimitAll, kSecReturnAttributes). Writes null-separated service names.

### keychain_linux.zig (libsecret)

- `store(service, account, data) !void`: libsecret SecretValue binary store via C bridge.
- `lookup(service, account, out_buf) !Result`: libsecret SecretValue binary lookup via C bridge.
- `delete(service, account) !void`: secret_password_clear_sync via C bridge.
- `search(account, out_buf, out_capacity) !usize`: secret_service_search_sync via C bridge.

## Build

```
zig build                        # static library -> zig-out/lib/
zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast # optimized build
zig build test                   # unit tests
zig build docs                   # generate API documentation
zig build example                # build C usage example
```

## Platform Support

- macOS 13+ (arm64, x86_64): Security.framework
- Linux (arm64, x86_64): libsecret-1-dev + libglib2.0-dev

## Apple / Swift / Objective-C Interop Scope

zig-keychain replaces only the generic-password keychain call site. The Apple
analogs are `SecItemAdd`, `SecItemCopyMatching`, `SecItemDelete`, and attribute
queries over `kSecClassGenericPassword`.

Not yet available: SwiftPM/modulemap packaging, ObjC nullability annotations,
dedicated Swift wrapper types, ObjC sample app, SecItem/libsecret migration
tables, access-control policies, biometric prompts, access groups,
synchronizable/iCloud items, certificates, private keys, or Secure Enclave
flows. Good starter contributions should focus on those interop and
documentation gaps before expanding the ABI.
